pr.title: Auto-regenerate the wiki Translation Units page in CI
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2585 added a GitHub Actions `update-wiki` job to `.github/workflows/build.yml` that runs after `build-ninja` on pushes to the default branch of `doldecomp/melee`, downloads the existing `GALE01_report` artifact, regenerates the wiki `Translation-Units.md` page with `tools/wiki_tu.py`, and pushes only when the page changes using the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write`.

key_files.role: Added the `update-wiki` GitHub Actions job for automatic Translation Units wiki regeneration.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: dependency installation review on this path

what_changed.text: Added an `update-wiki` GitHub Actions job that depends on `build-ninja`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: 'runs after a successful build (`needs: build-ninja`)'

what_changed.text: Restricted the job to pushes to the default branch of `doldecomp/melee`, skipping pull requests and forks.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: 'on push to the default branch of doldecomp/melee (skipped on PRs and forks)'

what_changed.text: Granted the job `contents: write` and `actions: read`, using `${{ github.token }}` as `GH_TOKEN` to clone and push to the repository wiki.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `permissions: contents: write`, `GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}`, wiki clone command

what_changed.text: Used sparse checkout for `tools` and `reqs`, installed generator dependencies with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt`, and downloaded the `GALE01_report` artifact to `report`.
Regenerated `Translation-Units.md` via `python tools/wiki_tu.py write report/report.json`, committed as `github-actions[bot]`, and skipped the push when `git diff --quiet` found no page changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bot git config and `git push origin HEAD:master`

smart_moves.text: Reused the existing `GALE01_report` artifact instead of adding another build step.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: 'reuses the existing `GALE01_report` artifact (no extra build)'

smart_moves.text: Scoped the automation to the canonical repository and default-branch pushes, reducing risk from forks or PR contexts.
Used a dedicated concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: false` so multiple master pushes do not race wiki pushes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: 'own `concurrency` group so master pushes can't race the wiki push'

smart_moves.text: Avoided a personal access token or machine user by validating that the built-in GitHub token can push to the repository's own wiki with `contents: write`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: 'No PAT, machine user, or GitHub App required'
text_corpus.jsonl: author comment says validation worked on fork with `${{ github.token }}`

smart_moves.text: Committed wiki changes only when the generated page differed, avoiding empty or noisy wiki commits.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: 'commits + pushes to the wiki only if the page changed'

decomp_lessons.lesson: For generated project status documentation, CI automation can reduce drift by regenerating from existing build artifacts after successful builds rather than requiring manual tool runs.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: '`tools/wiki_tu.py` generates the Translation Units wiki page, but nothing regenerates it automatically — it drifts until someone runs the tool by hand'

decomp_lessons.lesson: When CI pushes generated documentation, guard the job by event, branch, and repository so write-capable automation does not run on PRs or forks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: job-level `if` checks push, default branch, and `doldecomp/melee`

naming_conventions.name: update-wiki
naming_conventions.meaning: Job that updates the wiki Translation Units list.

naming_conventions.name: GH_TOKEN
naming_conventions.meaning: Shell variable bound to `${{ github.token }}` for authenticated wiki clone and push.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Requested installing generator dependencies with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` instead of pinned inline packages, with an optional suggestion to separate dependencies to reduce CI dependency footprint.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: Final diff uses `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` and sparse-checkout includes `reqs`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the token/wiki-push flow had been tested on a fork and discussed uncertainty around non-personal tokens.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: Author replied that no extra token was needed and linked a fork wiki and GitHub Actions check run as validation.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: review state CHANGES_REQUESTED body asks 'Have you tested it on your fork?'
human_pr_text.md: author comment links fork wiki and check run
human_pr_text.md: PR body says verified end-to-end on a fork

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Approved after follow-up; later comment thanked the author.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: Loaded text corpus shows an APPROVED review at 2026-06-06T11:39:50Z and issue comment 'Nice work on these, thanks.'
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: ribbanya comment 'Nice work on these, thanks.'
metadata_json: PR number, title, author, state, timestamps, changed file counts
human_pr_text.md: full PR body and issue comments
text_corpus.jsonl: PR body, issue comments, review comment, review states
notes: Evidence strongly supports the CI/documentation automation details because the full diff and PR body are loaded.
The PR is tooling/documentation-oriented, not a code decompilation match; assembly or objdiff tactics are not present.
The PR body claims fork validation and actionlint-clean status; intake records these as PR evidence, not independently rerun validation.
No source graph or current repository lookup was needed because the loaded PR slice answered the relevant questions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Automate generated decomp status docs from existing CI artifacts
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a repository status page is generated from build/report output, wiring the generator into CI after the successful build can prevent documentation drift without adding another build, as shown by reusing `GALE01_report` for `tools/wiki_tu.py`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: describes manual drift and CI regeneration goal

accepted_candidate_records.title: Gate write-capable wiki automation to canonical default-branch pushes
accepted_candidate_records.text: For GitHub Actions jobs that push generated wiki content, combine branch, event, and repository checks before granting write permissions, and skip commits when the generated output is unchanged.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: job-level `if` condition checks push/default branch/repository

source_update_candidates.title: Document CI pattern for generated wiki/status page updates
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that generated documentation jobs should reuse existing artifacts where possible, run only on canonical default-branch pushes, use the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` with the minimum required job permissions, serialize pushes with a concurrency group, and avoid commits when output is unchanged.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: PR body lists these design choices
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a project tooling convention candidate, not an existing decomp standard; curator should decide whether a CI/documentation source owns it.

rejection_notes: Do not treat this PR as evidence for assembly matching, objdiff, or source-code decomp tactics; it only changes GitHub Actions workflow configuration.
Do not promote the PR's fork-validation or actionlint-clean statements as independently verified by this intake; they are author-supplied PR evidence.
Do not generalize `contents: write` as sufficient for all wiki push cases beyond this repository's own wiki; the PR evidence is scoped to this repo/fork validation claim.
